title: Chapter 17
text: There never could be such a longing In the heart of a poor cowboy’s breast. As dwells in this heart you are breaking While I wait in my home in the West. Do you think of this valley you’re leaving, Oh, how lonely and dreary it will be? Do you think of the kind hearts you’re grieving, And the pain you are causing to me? From this valley they say you are going. I will miss your bright eyes and sweet smile. For they say you are weary and tired And must find a new range for a while.
